The '''''Meteor''-class Q-Carrier''' was a troop transport used by the [[True Mandalorians]], and later [[Mandalorian Protectors (Spar)|Mandalorian Protectors]].

==Characteristics==
[[File:Dropship.png|thumb|left|200px|Q-Carrier's landing in battle]]

Designed and produced by [[MandalMotors/Legends|MandalMotors]], the ''Meteor''-class acted as a drop ship capable of delivering Mandalorian soldiers quickly to the surface from the hangars of [[Mandalorian carrier]]s. The ships were reportedly shaped to resemble the faces of the original [[Taung/Legends|Taungs]].

In addition to heavy hull plating, the ship was protected with a twin [[Blaster cannon/Legends|blaster cannon]] on each side of the fuselage. Upon landing, four landing gear deployed capable of absorbing the shock of high-speed landings.

==History==
Its exclusive use by the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] earned it the name '''Mandalorian Drop Ship'''. It appeared based on similarly-designed dropships used by the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Neo-Crusaders]] during the [[Mandalorian Wars]].

After the [[Mandalorian Civil War (True Mandalorians)|Mandalorian Civil War]] disrupted MandalMotors, the ''Meteor''-class was replaced by the [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]] [[Amphibious Interstellar Assault Transport/infantry]].

In the final days of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]] in [[29 ABY/Legends|29 ABY]], [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] led a Mandalorian [[Mandalorian Supercommando|taskforce]] to reclaim the [[Yuuzhan Vong]]-held world of [[Gyndine/Legends|Gyndine]]. The force used ''Meteor''-class ships to transport soldiers to the planet's surface and were able to liberate the [[Planet/Legends|planet]].
